title: rosa carolina subsp. carolina
intro: Rosa carolina subsp. carolina, also known as the Carolina rose or wild rose, is a perennial shrub native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its pink to white flowers and fragrant, rose-petal-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rosa carolina subsp. carolina include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, salves, and infusions, and are often used to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and promote overall well-being.
